categories.name: Trigger Timing & Polling
categories.description: Zapier trigger delays vs HubSpot webhook real-time events

categories.name: Field Passthrough & Naming
categories.description: How HubSpot field names map to Zapier output field labels and how data types change

categories.name: Data Type Coercion
categories.description: Zapier coerces data types (numbers→strings, dates→formatted strings) before passing to downstream actions

categories.name: Rate Limit Delegation
categories.description: Zapier rate limiting behavior when acting as middleware between HubSpot and other APIs

mappings.toolBValue: Zapier polling: every 1–15 minutes (Free: 15min, Pro: 2min, Team: 1min)
mappings.notes: Zapier uses polling triggers for HubSpot (except specific webhook-supported triggers). A contact created in HubSpot may take up to 15 minutes to appear in Zapier. Developers expecting real-time behavior get confused when downstream actions don't fire immediately. This delay is not documented in Zapier's UI — only in fine print.
mappings.fixStrategy: Use HubSpot webhooks directly for real-time needs. Accept Zapier polling delay for non-time-sensitive flows. Use Zapier premium plan (1-min poll) for tighter requirements.

mappings.fieldName: HubSpot to Zapier Field Name Transformation
mappings.notes: Zapier transforms HubSpot property names to human-readable labels (firstname → 'First Name', hs_object_id → 'Object ID (hubspot)'). This is usually fine, but when using a 'Code by Zapier' or 'Webhooks by Zapier' step, the field naming can differ from the HubSpot API response format, causing confusion in downstream mapping.
mappings.fixStrategy: Use 'Code by Zapier' with Run JavaScript to fetch raw HubSpot API response if you need original field names. Otherwise, use Zapier's field picker to map correctly.

mappings.toolAValue: HubSpot types: number (int), datetime (ISO 8601), boolean (true/false), enumeration (string)
mappings.toolBValue: Zapier output: all fields are strings (text) unless explicitly typed in Zapier schema
mappings.notes: Zapier converts most HubSpot values to strings before passing to downstream actions. A HubSpot number (e.g., 'amount': 1000) becomes '1000' (string) in Zapier. If the downstream action expects a number (like ActiveCampaign deal value), it may fail or produce wrong data. Boolean becomes 'true'/'false' (string). DateTime becomes a formatted string depending on Zapier's formatting.
mappings.fixStrategy: In downstream actions that expect specific types, use Zapier's Formatter step to convert types explicitly. For Code by Zapier, use parseInt() or parseFloat() to restore numeric types.

mappings.toolAValue: HubSpot rate limit: 110 req/10s per account (public OAuth apps)
mappings.toolBValue: Zapier rate limit: depends on plan (Free: 100 tasks/mo, Pro: 750 tasks/mo, Team: 2000 tasks/mo)
mappings.notes: Zapier's task limit is its own rate limiter — not HubSpot's. A single HubSpot trigger can create many Zapier tasks if multiple downstream actions are chained. HubSpot rate limits apply per-account: if multiple Zaps share the same HubSpot connection, they collectively consume the 110 req/10s limit. Zapier doesn't coordinate rate limit consumption across Zaps.
mappings.fixStrategy: Monitor HubSpot API usage from Zapier connections. If hitting 429 errors in Zaps, throttle Zap frequency or upgrade HubSpot app limits.

errors.description: Zapier polls HubSpot every 1-15 minutes. Real-time data is not possible via standard Zapier triggers.
errors.fixSummary: Use HubSpot webhooks directly for real-time. Accept polling delay for non-critical flows.

errors.description: Zapier converts HubSpot numbers, booleans, and dates to strings. Downstream actions may fail or produce wrong data.
errors.fixSummary: Use Zapier Formatter step or Code by Zapier to restore original types.

errors.description: Zapier renames HubSpot internal property names to human-readable labels, causing mapping confusion in advanced Zaps.
errors.fixSummary: Use Code by Zapier with raw API for original field names. Use field picker for standard flows.
